papergraph.title: Multi‐Layer Charge Regulation Engineering for Efficient Enhancement of Aramid Epoxy‐Based Composite at Withstand Voltage Performance
papergraph.citation: Guowei Xia et al. (2026) Multi‐Layer Charge Regulation Engineering for Efficient Enhancement of Aramid Epoxy‐Based Composite at Withstand Voltage Performance doi:10.1002/adfm.76312
papergraph.venue: Advanced Functional Materials
papergraph.abstract: In recent years, fiber‐reinforced epoxy resin‐based composites have become commonly used for insulating components in high‐voltage transmission and transformation equipment. The high withstand voltage performance of materials is crucial to ensuring the safe and stable operation of equipment and the power grid. Therefore, in this paper, a multi‐layer charge regulation engineering was designed on the aramid fiber's surface to regulate the distribution and transmission of charges, thereby achieving an efficient enhancement of the withstand voltage of the composites. This engineering utilizes the charge trapping characteristics of insulating materials and the charge transport properties of semiconductor materials. The effectiveness of this engineering was validated through the tests of flashover voltage and breakdown strength. After constructing the optimal multi‐layer charge regulation structure, the flashover voltage increased by 62.03%, and the breakdown strength improved by 65.22%. Combining charge dissipation and trap distribution, interface bond strength, molecular dynamics simulations, and phase‐field modeling results reveals the mechanism of the multi‐layer charge regulation engineering. This can be summarized as the charge trapping in the F‐Group layer, uniform charge distribution in the ZnO nanowire layer, and deep charge transport in the FePc layer, which together ensure the effective enhancement of the material's withstand voltage performance.

The high withstand voltage performance of materials is crucial to ensuring the safe and stable operation of equipmen...[truncated]

papergraph.title: Testing Methods for Ultra-High Altitude Environmental Adaptability of Power Transmission and Transformation Sensing Terminals
papergraph.citation: Tongbo He et al. (2025) Testing Methods for Ultra-High Altitude Environmental Adaptability of Power Transmission and Transformation Sensing Terminals doi:10.1109/ICEEPS66790.2025.11239566
papergraph.venue: 2025 4th International Conference on Energy and Electrical Power Systems (ICEEPS)
papergraph.abstract: As core equipment for grid condition monitoring, power transmission and transformation sensing terminals face critical challenges in ultra-high altitude extreme environments, including material performance degradation, abnormal electrical characteristics, and inadequate operational adaptability. Existing environmental testing standards demonstrate limitations in simulating multi-stress coupled scenarios due to insufficient simulation capabilities and outdated control strategies. This paper proposes an ultra-high altitude adaptability testing methodology for power transmission sensing terminals based on multidimensional environmental coupling loading and dynamic gradient control. Through retrofitting a comprehensive test chamber with integrated ultraviolet irradiation, air circulation, and low-pressure control modules, we established a synchronized multi-environmental factor loading system. The implementation of a pressure-temperature-ultraviolet linkage algorithm coupled with a dynamic gradient control model enables precise simulation of altitude gradient variations. Experimental results demonstrate the method's effectiveness in replicating multidimensional extreme conditions: low pressure (≤70 kPa), intense ultraviolet radiation (≥1000 W/m2), wide temperature fluctuations (-40°C to 70°C), and strong winds (≥15 m/s), while significantly reducing testing cycle duration. This research provides technical support for reliability evaluation of power transmission equipment in ultra-high altitude regions such as the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau, demonstrating substantial engineering application value.

papergraph.title: Consensus-based feature selection and Bayesian-optimized stacking ensembles for comprehensive fault diagnosis in series-compensated ultra-high-voltage power systems
papergraph.citation: Rab Nawaz et al. (2026) Consensus-based feature selection and Bayesian-optimized stacking ensembles for comprehensive fault diagnosis in series-compensated ultra-high-voltage power systems doi:10.1038/s41598-026-49195-7
papergraph.venue: Scientific Reports
papergraph.abstract: Modern power systems incorporate ultra-high-voltage transmission lines with series compensation to enhance their reliability and stability. These configurations generate complex data patterns in electrical signals, which present challenges for accurate fault diagnosis. This complexity arises from various potential faults, such as high-impedance faults and inductive-capacitive interactions, compounded by data scarcity owing to privacy and cybersecurity constraints. This study employs a simulation-based analysis to facilitate fault detection, classification, and localization as unified Multi-Classification tasks. The methodology improves the time-frequency domain data through meta-feature engineering and utilizes consensus-based feature selection by combining gradient boosting, variance thresholding, and mutual information. Model learning is conducted using Bayesian optimization within a Stacking Ensemble, integrating Extra Trees, Light Gradient Boosting Machine, and Extreme Gradient Boosting with an optimized Random Forest meta-classifier. The evaluation considers the accuracy, computational time, inference speed, and memory usage under challenging conditions to address practical limitations. The results indicate that the Stacking Ensemble achieves the highest performance despite its greater computational demand. Extra Trees offers the fastest execution but shows decreased accuracy with increased classification complexity. The Light Gradient Boosting Machine and Extreme Gradient Boosting demonstrate optimized performance across tasks owing to enhanced data processing. These findings confirm the practical applicability of the proposed fault diagnosis framework to modern ultra-high-voltage power systems.

papergraph.title: Research on Wireless Power Transmission Technology and Its Application in Electrical Engineering
papergraph.citation: Xiaolong Wang et al. (2025) Research on Wireless Power Transmission Technology and Its Application in Electrical Engineering doi:10.1109/ICPICS66386.2025.11347183
papergraph.venue: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Power, Intelligent Computing and Systems (ICPICS)
papergraph.abstract: In order to improve the flexibility and safety of power supply of electrical engineering systems in scenarios such as high-voltage isolation, complex environments and mobile devices, this paper studies wireless power transmission technology and its application in smart substations, comprehensively and systematically sorts out three types of wireless power supply mechanisms: electromagnetic induction, magnetic resonance coupling and microwave transmission, builds a wireless power supply system framework with magnetic resonance coupling as the core, and deploys the system in 110 kV smart substation scenarios. Test results show that the system achieves an average transmission efficiency of 78.6 % at a coupling distance of 2.8 meters, controls the power fluctuation rate within plus or minus 2.6 %, and reduces the annual interruption frequency to 3 times. The comprehensive operation stability and safety far exceed the traditional wired method. Wireless power transmission shows good engineering practice feasibility and expansion potential, and is suitable for key parts in smart grids that have high requirements for flexibility, safety and efficiency of energy supply.

papergraph.title: Study on the Safety of Power Frequency Electro-Magnetic Exposure from Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines
papergraph.citation: Ji-kun Guo (2024) Study on the Safety of Power Frequency Electro-Magnetic Exposure from Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines doi:10.1109/APPEEC61255.2024.10922335
papergraph.venue: IEEE PES Asia-Pacific Power and Energy Engineering Conference
papergraph.abstract: To prevent potential electromagnetic hazards associated with electrical equipment, the method of employing electromag-netic exposure limits is a widely accepted technical specification across various countries. This study utilizes a 1:1 human body model positioned under a 550 kV ultra-high voltage transmission line to in-vestigate the effects of electromagnetic exposure at different locations. The results indicate that within the power frequency electromagnetic influence zone of the transmission line, there are instances where exposure levels approach or exceed the 10 kV/m limit. However, due to the shielding effect of the human body's weak conductivity, the maximum induced electric field strength and current density at various body parts from the 550 kV ultra-high voltage transmission line were found to be only 62.665 mV/m and 487.943 11 Alm2, respectively. These values are significantly lower than the significant hazard thresholds of 100 m VIm and 2000 11 Alm2 established in biomedical standards. Moreover, as the distance from the center of the transmission lines increases, electromagnetic exposure decays rapidly. There-fore, despite the issue of power frequency electromagnetic exposure from high-voltage and ultra-high voltage transmission lines approaching or exceeding technical regulation limits, the actual harm to humans remains relatively low.

papergraph.title: System optimization of long-distance energy transportation in China using ultra-high-voltage power transmission
papergraph.citation: Yaru Zhang et al. (2018) System optimization of long-distance energy transportation in China using ultra-high-voltage power transmission doi:10.1063/1.5013177
papergraph.venue: Journal of Renewable and Sustainable Energy
papergraph.abstract: Energy transportation is an important issue for China's electricity system due to the unbalanced distribution of energy sources and electrical demands. The traditional method of energy transportation (e.g., coal transportation) is challenging due to both the long distance of transportation and the large amount of energy that it requires. In recent years, China has been developing ultra-high voltage (UHV) as an alternative way of transmitting energy over a long distance, and many UHV lines are being planned for construction during the 13th Five-Year Plan (FYP) period. This paper develops a system optimization model for exploring the optimal pathway for long-distance energy transportation in China during the 13th FYP period from the perspective of the minimum cost of an energy transportation system. The results show that (a) although UHV transmission will be increasingly adopted in the 13th FYP period, coal transportation will still play an important role; (b) eight UHV lines are suggested to be built and put into use as a priority in the 13th FYP period; (c) the high technology learning effect, electricity demand, and investment costs of power plants would promote the adoption of UHV. Based on these results, some policy suggestions are provided.

papergraph.scenario: long-distance energy transportation in China
papergraph.benchmark: minimum cost of an energy transportation system
papergraph.contribution: Developed a system optimization model for exploring the optimal pathway for long-distance energy transportation in China, suggesting eight UHV lines to be prioritized and providing policy recommendations.

papergraph.title: Development of High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) Transmission Systems for Efficient Power Transfer
papergraph.citation: Naveen Garg et al. (2025) Development of High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) Transmission Systems for Efficient Power Transfer doi:10.1109/CISES66934.2025.11264962
papergraph.venue: 2025 International Conference on Intelligent and Secure Engineering Solutions (CISES)
papergraph.abstract: The direct transmission systems using the voltage of one of the largest values, HVDC, that allow transmitting electricity with high efficiency and safety over long distances may be considered one of the most important milestones in the history of electricity engineering and development of the electrical power systems. It has a few strengths such as; it has a lower power loss compared with standard alternating current (AC) systems, is more stable on the grid, and has power density. The fundamental units of the HVDC technology are transformer stations, lines and control systems. HVDC converters technology has made the major focus on voltage source converters (VSCs) and line-commutated converters (LCCs) in power conversion between AC and DC to achieve maximum efficiency of the system. Renewable energy: Here HVDC can adopt renewable energy sources like wind power and solar energy to ease transfer of power through modular and long-distance power transmission of remote generation sites to the centre of consumption. Technology HVDC is being developed in the direction of higher converter efficiency, reduced cost and decreased fault tolerance. Enhanced control algorithms and real-time monitoring software guarantee dynamic stability and easy interface with current grid infrastructures. With the information on the cleaner energy future, an even further progress of the HVDC systems that will be incorporated in the world energy transition on the way to more sustainable and resilient electricity power systems is proceeding.

papergraph.title: Application of Integrated Optical Electric-Field Sensor on the Measurements of Transient Voltages in AC High-Voltage Power Grids
papergraph.citation: S. Xie et al. (2019) Application of Integrated Optical Electric-Field Sensor on the Measurements of Transient Voltages in AC High-Voltage Power Grids doi:10.3390/APP9091951
papergraph.venue: Applied Sciences
papergraph.abstract: Transient voltages in the power grid are the key for the fault analysis of a power grid, optimized insulation design, and the standardization of the high-voltage testing method. The traditional measuring equipment, based on electrical engineering, normally has a limited bandwidth and response speed, which are also featured by a huge size and heavy weight. In this paper, an integrated optical electric-field sensor based on the Pockels effect was developed and applied to measure the transient voltages on the high-voltage conductors in a non-contact measuring mode. The measuring system has a response speed faster than 6 ns and a wide bandwidth ranging from 5 Hz to 100 MHz. Moreover, the sensors have the dimensions of 18 mm by 18 mm by 48 mm and a light weight of dozens of grams. The measuring systems were employed to monitor the lightning transient voltages on a 220 kV overhead transmission line. The switching transient voltages were also measured by the measuring system during the commissioning of the 500 kV middle Tibet power grid. In 2017, 307 lightning transient voltages caused by induction stroke were recorded. The characteristics of these voltage waveforms are different from the standard lightning impulse voltage proposed by IEC standards. Three types of typical switching transient voltage in 500 kV AC power grid were measured, and the peak values of these overvoltages can reach 1.73 times rated voltage.
papergraph.method: integrated optical electric-field sensor based on the Pockels effect
papergraph.scenario: measurements of transient voltages in AC high-voltage power grids
papergraph.benchmark: lightning and switching transient voltages
papergraph.contribution: development and application of an integrated optical electric-field sensor for measuring transient voltages in high-voltage power grids with improved response speed and bandwidth

papergraph.title: Research on system optimization and strategic path of China’s energy security from the perspective of electrical engineering
papergraph.citation: D. Danylchenko et al. (2025) Research on system optimization and strategic path of China’s energy security from the perspective of electrical engineering doi:10.20998/eree.2025.1(10).333342
papergraph.venue: Bulletin of the National Technical University "KhPI". Series: Energy: Reliability and Energy Efficiency
papergraph.abstract: The article presents a comprehensive study of China's energy security from a technical and economic perspective, taking into account current challenges and strategic goals of the energy transition. The main focus is on the quantitative analysis of structural problems that threaten the stability of the energy system. In particular, based on 2023 data, it was found that the structure of energy consumption remains dependent on fossil fuels: the share of coal is 56.4 %, and oil import dependence is 72.6 %. In addition, the probability of overloading the power grid in extreme weather conditions was found to be 7.3 %, indicating the system's vulnerability to climate change. The study modeled the effectiveness of the implementation of Smart Grid technologies, which increased the efficiency of ultra-high voltage power transmission by 18 %. The study also recorded a 40 % increase in the total capacity of virtual power plants and an increase in the internal rate of return of energy storage systems based on lithium batteries to 8.7 %. From the perspective of long-term strategic planning, the proposed multi-energy flow model estimates the cost of transporting 500 km of green hydrogen at 35 yuan/kg, and also forms a standardized power compensation mechanism (0.08 yuan/kW). The empirical results confirm the feasibility of such measures: the rate of rejection of new transmission lines in the northwestern regions has been reduced to 3.2 %, and the introduction of 12 GW of energy storage systems in the Yangtze Delta has reduced peak load by 15%. It is also demonstrated that the cost of hydrogen production from offshore wind energy has reached 0.3 yuan/kWh, or 35 yuan/kg, taking into account market electricity tariffs. The article proposes strategic measures to ensure energy security, including investments in scientific and technological development in the amount of 0.8 % of gross domestic product, modernization of the power grid with funding of 4.2 trillion yuan over the next 10 years, and the creation of an adaptive political monitoring system. These results form the basis for a systematic electrical engineering approach to ensuring China's energy sustainability in the context of global transformations.

papergraph.title: Comparing a Multi-Terminal High Voltage Direct Current and Unified Power Flow Control for Short Circuit Level Reduction in Iraqi 400 Kv Grid
papergraph.citation: A. N. Merzah et al. (2024) Comparing a Multi-Terminal High Voltage Direct Current and Unified Power Flow Control for Short Circuit Level Reduction in Iraqi 400 Kv Grid doi:10.51244/ijrsi.2024.1107018
papergraph.venue: International journal of research and scientific innovation
papergraph.abstract: The demand for electricity has increased significantly in recent years; yet, the transmission and distribution networks are unable to meet unrestrained demands because of resource constraint. Losses in power lines exacerbate the unfavorable conditions for maximum power transfer. Recent decades have seen a rise in the use of FACTS and High Voltage Direct Current transmission in power systems due to its many advantages, which include reduced short circuit level (SCL), enhanced voltage profiles, reduced network power losses, and enhanced system reliability and safety. The paper presents a comparing study of the effectiveness of Unified Power Flow Control (UPFC) and Multi-Terminal High Voltage Direct Current (MT-HVDC) transmission in reducing short circuit current and network power losses for the Iraqi Super High Voltage (ISHV) grid. Modeling of Unified Power Flow Control and Multi-Terminal High Voltage Direct Current transmission is carried out using the Power System Simulator for Engineering (PSS/E) version 32 Package Program. The integration of multi-terminal HVDC transmission to the system resulted in a considerable decrease in short circuit levels and network power losses for the majority of buses in the system, as well as a reduction in the loading on the transmission lines in the southern region of Iraqi Super High Voltage grid, As a result, the South’s network performance will improve, hence improving Iraq’s electrical power system. Furthermore, when compared to installing a UPFC, the results showed that multi-terminal HVDC transmission reduces short circuit levels more efficiently.

papergraph.title: Technical Transformation Scheme and Practice Applied in Ultra-high Share of Renewable Energy Power System at County-level Scale
papergraph.citation: Ping Xiong et al. (2025) Technical Transformation Scheme and Practice Applied in Ultra-high Share of Renewable Energy Power System at County-level Scale doi:10.1109/ICPST65050.2025.11088518
papergraph.venue: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Power Science and Technology (ICPST)
papergraph.abstract: The rapid integration of variable renewable energy into county-level power grids has introduced significant operational challenges, particularly transmission congestion and transformer overload caused by bi-directional flow. To overcome the restriction of conventional grid reinforcement solutions focused mainly on infrastructure expansion, the work develops an integrated generation-grid-load-storage coordination framework adapted to scenarios with high penetration of renewables. The solution achieves localized energy balance through optimized energy storage allocation and operational modes control, establishing a resilient power grid architecture capable of grid-connected interaction and autonomous islanded operation. Renovation schemes of building a power system with ultra-high renewable penetration ratios are presented, in which technical routes for the coordination framework are investigated. Performance of key technologies, including grid-forming voltage source converters, smooth mode transition, and the collective control of the coordination system in support of the independent operation and stability of a 100% variable inverter-based renewable energy sources power system, is validated.

papergraph.title: Implications of GB/T 22384 Revision for Standardized Testing of Power Grid Security and Stability Control Systems
papergraph.citation: Lei Fu et al. (2025) Implications of GB/T 22384 Revision for Standardized Testing of Power Grid Security and Stability Control Systems doi:10.1109/ICPST65050.2025.11089103
papergraph.venue: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Power Science and Technology (ICPST)
papergraph.abstract: The revision of China’s national standard GB/T 22384 addresses critical challenges posed by new-type power grids, characterized by large-scale renewable energy integration, ultra-high voltage AC/DC transmission, and cyber-physical system complexities. Aligned with China’s "dual carbon" strategy and the technical demands of New Power Systems, this revision introduces expanded testing scopes, enhanced network security protection requirements, and refined dynamic simulation methodologies. A case study of the Qing-Yu HVDC project demonstrates the effectiveness of closed-loop test based on real-time digital simulation (RTDS) in validating hierarchical security and stability control systems (SSCS), revealing latent defects such as redundant tripping commands during cascading faults. Innovations lie in integrating RTDS for closed-loop validation of multi-scenario strategies, introducing dynamic state synchronization to mitigate coordination failures, and optimizing lifecycle testing management. The revised standard establishes a robust framework for standardized testing, ensuring reliability and adaptability of SSCS in high-renewable, power electronics-dominated grids. From the main aspects of this revision and the results of engineering empirical tests, the testing methods and technical requirements for SSCS should evolve iteratively in sync with the development process and technological advancements of power systems. SSCS plays a crucial role in ensuring the secure and stable operation of power systems, and the standardization work cannot be achieved just in one effort.

papergraph.title: Research on a Four-Layer Intelligent Monitoring and Coordination Model for Ultra-High Voltage Transmission and Substation Stations in Xinjiang
papergraph.citation: Jiangtao Guo et al. (2025) Research on a Four-Layer Intelligent Monitoring and Coordination Model for Ultra-High Voltage Transmission and Substation Stations in Xinjiang doi:10.1109/ICEPG67373.2025.11466551
papergraph.venue: 2025 IEEE 7th International Conference on Energy, Power and Grid (ICEPG)
papergraph.abstract: In response to the unique challenges posed by the rapid expansion of Xinjiang's ultra-high voltage (UHV) power transmission and transformation network, including vast geographical areas, harsh environmental conditions, and a high proportion of renewable energy, this paper proposes a four-layer intelligent monitoring and collaborative framework for future ultra-high voltage digital substations. The proposed framework integrates the perception layer, the edge computing layer, the intelligent analysis layer, and the collaborative decision-making layer. The objective is to achieve multi-source data collection, real-time energy flow visualization, predictive maintenance, resilience assessment, and multi-station collaborative control. The perception layer deploys a distributed sensor network to facilitate continuous monitoring. The edge computing layer undertakes low-latency local processing and anomaly detection. The intelligent analysis layer employs machine learning models to calculate health indices, forecast loads and optimize operations. The collaborative decision-making layer facilitates cross-station coordination, scheduling and emergency management. This framework provides a comprehensive technical and functional reference for the large-scale deployment of digital substation technology in Xinjiang and similar regions.

papergraph.title: Research on Reactive Power and Voltage Layered and Partitioned Collaborative Control Strategy for Suppressing the Chain Evolution of Large Scale New Energy DC Transmission
papergraph.citation: Tao Sun et al. (2025) Research on Reactive Power and Voltage Layered and Partitioned Collaborative Control Strategy for Suppressing the Chain Evolution of Large Scale New Energy DC Transmission doi:10.1109/ETEP67941.2025.11440636
papergraph.venue: 2025 2nd International Conference on Energy Technology and Electrical Power (ETEP)
papergraph.abstract: Large scale new energy ultra-high voltage direct current transmission, due to the low short-circuit ratio of the transmission end power grid and the strong impact of DC commutation failure, causes significant transient voltage fluctuations along the propagation path of the transmission end power grid from the DC rectifier station to the new energy cluster stations to the new energy collection stations, which in turn triggers a chain evolution of new energy high and low voltage crossing protection actions to disconnect from the grid, impacting the safety of the power grid. This article proposes a reactive power voltage layered and partitioned collaborative control strategy for multiple reactive power sources in DC rectifier station, new energy cluster stations, and new energy collection stations to prevent and control this problem. Considering the time delay of SVG power electronic response control in new energy collection stations, an SVG high and low voltage open-loop control strategy is proposed. Compared with closed-loop PID control, it can enhance the timeliness of control and effectively suppress the rapid transient voltage fluctuations caused by commutation failure. At the same time, the simulation analysis of the layered and partitioned dynamic support and rapid regulation effect provided by the rectifier station synchronous condenser and the thermal power units in the near area of new energy cluster stations is conducted. Based on simulation examples of actual power grids, the effectiveness of the proposed multi reactive power source layered and partitioned collaborative control strategy in suppressing the cascading evolution problem of the transmission end power grid has been verified, improving the DC transmission capacity and promoting the consumption of new energy.
papergraph.method: reactive power voltage layered and partitioned collaborative control strategy
papergraph.scenario: large scale new energy ultra-high voltage direct current transmission
papergraph.benchmark: transient voltage fluctuations, DC transmission capacity, new energy consumption
papergraph.contribution: a reactive power and voltage layered and partitioned collaborative control strategy that enhances the timeliness of control and effectively suppresses the rapid transient voltage fluctuations caused by commutation failure, thereby improving the DC transmission capacity and promoting the consumption of new energy
papergraph.limitation: Considering the time delay of SVG power electronic response control in new energy collection stations, an SVG high and low voltage open-loop control strategy is proposed.

papergraph.title: Investigation into In Situ Computed Tomography Inspection Technology for Conductor Connection Structure of Ultra-High Voltage Gas-Insulated Transmission Lines
papergraph.citation: Chao Li et al. (2025) Investigation into In Situ Computed Tomography Inspection Technology for Conductor Connection Structure of Ultra-High Voltage Gas-Insulated Transmission Lines doi:10.1134/S1061830925600133
papergraph.venue: Russian journal of nondestructive testing
papergraph.abstract: The conductor connection structure in gas-insulated transmission lines (GIL) is vital for power grid stability. Traditional inspections require power outages or fault signal detection post-failure, lacking real-time internal structure assessment. This paper introduces an in situ computed Tomography (CT) inspection technology for ultra-high voltage GIL conductors, aiming for non-destructive, high-precision, real-time monitoring. It analyzes Yuyang line GIL’s inspection needs and designs a system with a ring-clamping mechanism and adaptable scanning path. A specialized algorithm tackles incomplete data and imaging accuracy issues unique to GIL. Experiments show the technology’s ability to image GIL conductor connections in three-dimensional (3D), detect defects, and deformations, crucial for GIL’s safe operation and maintenance by identifying and preventing potential failures.
papergraph.method: in situ computed tomography (CT) inspection technology
papergraph.scenario: ultra-high voltage gas-insulated transmission lines (GIL) conductor connection structure
papergraph.benchmark: defect and deformation detection
papergraph.contribution: introduction of a non-destructive, high-precision, real-time monitoring system for GIL conductor connections using in situ CT technology
papergraph.limitation: Investigation into In Situ Computed Tomography Inspection Technology for Conductor Connection Structure of Ultra-High Voltage Gas-Insulated Transmission Lines The conductor connection structure in gas-insulated transmission lines (GIL) is vital for power grid stability.

papergraph.title: Study on Power Supply Reliability of Sending End Matching Power Access to Ultra-high Voltage AC and DC Hybrid Power Grid
papergraph.citation: Zheng Bowen et al. (2018) Study on Power Supply Reliability of Sending End Matching Power Access to Ultra-high Voltage AC and DC Hybrid Power Grid doi:10.1109/CICED.2018.8592243
papergraph.venue: China International Conference on Electricity Distribution
papergraph.abstract: With the continuous operation of ultra-high voltage (UHV) AC and DC power transmission and transformation projects in Inner Mongolia autonomous region, Inner Mongolia power grid has become a UHV AC and DC hybrid system. However, the development of AC and DC projects is not balanced, and the characteristics of “strong DC and weak AC” are obvious, It is bound to affect the safe and stable operation of UHV power grids, when sending end matching thermal power units connecting to UHV AC and DC hybrid power grid, the reliability of power supply is difficult to be guaranteed. Taking the sending end regional power grid of Xilinhot to Taizhou (Jiangsu Province) UHV DC transmission project (hereinafter referred to as Xitai DC) as the research object, by using Power System Analysis Software Package simulation software, this paper deeply analyzes the influence of high-power matching power supply on the safe and stable operation of the power grid after connecting to the UHV AC and DC hybrid power grid, and put forward the corresponding prevention and control measures.
papergraph.method: Power System Analysis Software Package simulation
papergraph.scenario: Ultra-high voltage AC and DC hybrid power grid
papergraph.benchmark: safe and stable operation of the power grid
papergraph.contribution: Analysis of the influence of high-power matching power supply on the safe and stable operation of the UHV AC and DC hybrid power grid and proposed prevention and control measures

papergraph.title: A Simulation Study on Grounding Faults in the Metal Shield Layer of High-Voltage Power Cables
papergraph.citation: Zifan Zhang et al. (2025) A Simulation Study on Grounding Faults in the Metal Shield Layer of High-Voltage Power Cables doi:10.1109/ICLCSE66889.2025.11389670
papergraph.venue: 2025 International Conference on Low Carbon and Smart Energy (ICLCSE)
papergraph.abstract: In the context of accelerating urbanisation and the transformation of energy structures, high voltage power cables have emerged as a pivotal transmission method within contemporary power systems. The operational reliability of these entities has been demonstrated to have a direct impact on grid security and power supply quality. Within cable structures, the metallic shielding layer fulfils a dual function: it both suppresses electromagnetic interference and protects communication facilities, while also providing a safe return path for ground faults and preventing electric shock accidents. The present study investigates the mechanisms and characteristics of short-circuit faults in cable shields. Utilising the electromagnetic transient simulation software PSCAD, a refined cable model incorporating frequency-dependent parameters was constructed. The electrical characteristics and influencing factors of such faults were revealed through analysis of the variation patterns of shield currents and zero-sequence currents under different operating conditions. The present research provides both theoretical and empirical support for the rapid diagnosis and condition assessment in engineering applications.
papergraph.method: electromagnetic transient simulation using PSCAD
papergraph.scenario: grounding faults in the metal shield layer of high-voltage power cables
papergraph.benchmark: shield currents and zero-sequence currents
papergraph.contribution: revealing the electrical characteristics and influencing factors of grounding faults in high-voltage power cable shields
papergraph.limitation: A Simulation Study on Grounding Faults in the Metal Shield Layer of High-Voltage Power Cables In the context of accelerating urbanisation and the transformation of energy structures, high voltage power cables have emerged as a pivotal transmission method within contemporary power systems.

papergraph.title: Synoptic Characteristics of a Continuous Ice Covering Event on Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines in 2023
papergraph.citation: Xinyi Fu et al. (2024) Synoptic Characteristics of a Continuous Ice Covering Event on Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines in 2023 doi:10.1109/eeps63402.2024.10804497
papergraph.venue: 2024 4th International Conference on Energy Engineering and Power Systems (EEPS)
papergraph.abstract: Yunnan is an important channel for the transmission of power from west to east of China Southern Power Grid. During January 14–21, 2023, a large-scale continuous ice covering event occurred in Qujing area of Yunnan Province, with the maximum ice covering thickness of 39.71mm. Based on the reanalysis data of the European Center for Medium Range Weather Prediction (ECMWF) and the observation data of the network of meteorological observation stations, combined with the ice-covering records of ultra-high voltage transmission lines of China Southern Power Grid, the synoptic causes of the ice-covering of power lines are comprehensively analyzed from the aspects of weather situation, atmospheric stratification, local meteorological elements and atmospheric circulation index. The results show that during the glaciating process, the Okhotsk Sea cold vortex is maintained and the transverse trough is turned to vertical. The blocking high leads the northerly dry and cold air in front of the ridge to move southward. Under the influence of the western Pacific tropical high and the southern branch trough, the warm and wet air in front of the rear ridge meets the cold and warm air in front of the ridge. The advance and retreat of the quasi-stationary front determines the icing process of the wire. That is, when the cold air is strong and full of water vapor, the overcooled raindrops freeze or fog drops condenses to form the icing process of the wire. The ice on the wires melts naturally under the warm air mass.

papergraph.scenario: ice covering event on ultra-high voltage transmission lines

papergraph.abstract: This paper analyzes the causes of lightning faults in ultra-high voltage transmission lines of the power grid, including discharge traces and channels, grounding resistance detection, lightning resistance level simulation, and lightning resistance level calculation, in order to obtain the causes of lightning faults. Based on the analysis of lightning faults, six governance measures to avoid lightning faults are proposed, providing reference for reducing the frequency of lightning faults in ultra-high voltage transmission lines.

papergraph.title: Research on the Protection Scheme of a High-Speed Railway Crossing 1000 KV Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Line
papergraph.citation: Yi-chun Song et al. (2024) Research on the Protection Scheme of a High-Speed Railway Crossing 1000 KV Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Line doi:10.3390/infrastructures9070110
papergraph.venue: Infrastructures
papergraph.abstract: The high-speed railway project and the ultra-high-voltage transmission project represent two crucial components of China’s “new infrastructure”. As the construction of these two projects progresses rapidly, it is inevitable that instances of intersections will occur. Extreme conditions may cause damage to ultra-high voltage transmission cables. When a high-speed train passes by an ultra-high voltage transmission line, it poses a serious safety hazard. To address this issue, engineering examples were utilized to examine the protection structure scheme, protection distance, protection load, and construction procedures when a high-speed railway intersects a 1000 KV ultra-high voltage transmission line. A shed structure form and construction method for the electric power protection were proposed to ensure the safe operation of the high-speed railway while also achieving the safe and rapid construction of the high-speed railway protection structure in the safety zone of the approaching 1000 kV ultra-high voltage transmission line. The results indicated that the protection of high-speed railway crossings and 1000 kV ultra-high voltage transmission lines primarily focuses on line-break protection. The concrete shed structure with a straight wall and a flat roof was designed to meet the requirements of high-speed railway crossings. The line-break protection method enables the construction of an automatic warning protection corridor and a complete movable trolley quickly and safely within the safety zone near the transmission line. The implementation effect is, therefore, positive. It can be used as a reference point for other projects of a similar nature.
papergraph.method: engineering examples, shed structure design, and construction method
papergraph.scenario: high-speed railway crossing 1000 kV ultra-high voltage transmission line
papergraph.benchmark: line-break protection, construction safety, and operational safety
papergraph.contribution: a concrete shed structure and construction method for protecting high-speed railways and ultra-high voltage transmission lines, enabling safe and rapid construction in the safety zone
papergraph.limitation: A shed structure form and construction method for the electric power protection were proposed to ensure the safe operation of the high-speed railway while also achieving the safe and rapid construction of the high-speed railway protection structure in the safety zone of the approaching 1000 kV ultra-high voltage transmission line.

papergraph.title: A Scheme of Controlled Switching System in Converter Station
papergraph.citation: Haitao Li et al. (2024) A Scheme of Controlled Switching System in Converter Station doi:10.1109/IMCEC59810.2024.10575086
papergraph.venue: IEEE Advanced Information Management, Communicates, Electronic and Automation Control Conference
papergraph.abstract: For the past few years, in the HVDC (high voltage direct current transmission) and UHVDC (ultra high voltage direct current transmission) projects, the circuit breaker closing resistor failure has occurred many times, which has adversely affected the safety and stability of operation. For the switching operation of AC filter and converter transformer in converter station, the controlled switching system can improve the on/off transient process of circuit breaker, as well as avoiding the impact on power equipment and power grid when the closing resistor is cancelled. Based on plenty of the implementation and testing experience of controlled switching system in converter stations, the composition of controlled switching system and technical requirements, circuit breaker matching and functional test are presented briefly. This paper focuses on the controlled switching applications of filter banks and converter transformers, summarizes the control strategy, the commissioning requirements of controlled switching system, and the key problems in engineering and equipment operation and maintenance, so as to provide reference for relevant technical personnel. A special transformation scheme of eliminating closing resistor and adding controlled switching is introduced in this paper. This scheme has been implemented in several projects and proven effective.

papergraph.title: Review of Power Electronic Solutions for Dielectric Barrier Discharge Applications
papergraph.citation: Hyeongmeen Baik et al. (2025) Review of Power Electronic Solutions for Dielectric Barrier Discharge Applications doi:10.1109/TPEL.2026.3658517
papergraph.venue: arXiv
papergraph.abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive review of dielectric barrier discharge (DBD) power supply topologies, aiming to bridge the gap between DBD applications and power electronics design. Two key aspects are examined: the dependence of the DBD electrical model on reactor geometry, and application-driven requirements for injected waveform characteristics, including shapes, voltage amplitude, frequency, and modulation techniques. On this basis, the paper systematically reviews two major categories of power supplies: sinusoidal types comprising transformerless and transformer-based resonant inverters, and pulsed power supplies (PPSs). The review summarizes performance trade-offs, highlights untested topologies and emerging applications, and offers guidance for advancing high-performance DBD power supply design for next-generation systems.
papergraph.method: comprehensive review
papergraph.scenario: dielectric barrier discharge (DBD) power supply topologies
papergraph.benchmark: performance trade-offs, untested topologies, and emerging applications
papergraph.contribution: bridging the gap between DBD applications and power electronics design, and offering guidance for high-performance DBD power supply design

keynotes.title: Multi‐Layer Charge Regulation Engineering for Efficient Enhancement of Aramid Epoxy‐Based Composite at Withstand Voltage Performance

keynote.title: Multi‐Layer Charge Regulation Engineering for Efficient Enhancement of Aramid Epoxy‐Based Composite at Withstand Voltage Performance
keynote.core_problem: Improving the withstand voltage performance of aramid epoxy-based composites used in high-voltage transmission and transformation equipment to ensure the safe and stable operation of such equipment and the power grid.
contributions: Designing a multi-layer charge regulation engineering on the aramid fiber's surface to regulate the distribution and transmission of charges, thereby enhancing the withstand voltage of the composites.
methods: Utilizing the charge trapping characteristics of insulating materials and the charge transport properties of semiconductor materials.
Constructing an optimal multi-layer charge regulation structure.
experiments_or_evidence: Tests of flashover voltage and breakdown strength were conducted to validate the effectiveness of the multi-layer charge regulation engineering.
Combining charge dissipation and trap distribution, interface bond strength, molecular dynamics simulations, and phase-field modeling to reveal the mechanism behind the enhancement.

gap_signals.text: The mechanism of the multi-layer charge regulation engineering is explained through charge trapping in the F-Group layer, uniform charge distribution in the ZnO nanowire layer, and deep charge transport in the FePc layer, indicating areas where further research could refine or expand this understanding.

datasets_or_materials: F-Group layer, ZnO nanowire layer, FePc layer

important_claims.claim: The flashover voltage increased by 62.03%, and the breakdown strength improved by 65.22% after constructing the optimal multi-layer charge regulation structure.
important_claims.evidence: Validation through tests of flashover voltage and breakdown strength.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: This study provides a foundational approach for enhancing the electrical insulation properties of composite materials, which can be applied to other similar materials and structures, potentially leading to improvements in high-voltage applications.

keynotes.title: Testing Methods for Ultra-High Altitude Environmental Adaptability of Power Transmission and Transformation Sensing Terminals

keynote.title: Testing Methods for Ultra-High Altitude Environmental Adaptability of Power Transmission and Transformation Sensing Terminals
keynote.core_problem: Power transmission and transformation sensing terminals face critical challenges in ultra-high altitude extreme environments, including material performance degradation, abnormal electrical characteristics, and inadequate operational adaptability. Existing testing standards are limited in simulating multi-stress coupled scenarios.
contributions: Proposed an ultra-high altitude adaptability testing methodology based on multidimensional environmental coupling loading and dynamic gradient control.

Retrofitted a comprehensive test chamber with integrated ultraviolet irradiation, air circulation, and low-pressure control modules to establish a synchronized multi-environmental factor loading system.
methods: Implemented a pressure-temperature-ultraviolet linkage algorithm coupled with a dynamic gradient control model to simulate altitude gradient variations.
experiments_or_evidence: Conducted experiments that demonstrated the method's effectiveness in replicating multidimensional extreme conditions: low pressure (≤70 kPa), intense ultraviolet radiation (≥1000 W/m2), wide temperature fluctuations (-40°C to 70°C), and strong winds (≥15 m/s).
Reduced testing cycle duration significantly.
assumptions: The retrofitted test chamber and the implemented algorithms can accurately simulate the multi-stress coupled scenarios found in ultra-high altitude environments.
limitations: The study focuses specifically on power transmission and transformation sensing terminals; applicability to other types of equipment may require further investigation.

gap_signals.text: The study focuses specifically on power transmission and transformation sensing terminals; applicability to other types of equipment may require further investigation.
Existing environmental testing standards demonstrate limitations in simulating multi-stress coupled scenarios due to insufficient simulation capabilities and outdated control strategies.

datasets_or_materials: Comprehensive test chamber with integrated ultraviolet irradiation, air circulation, and low-pressure control modules.
code_or_implementation: Pressure-temperature-ultraviolet linkage algorithm and dynamic gradient control model.

important_claims.claim: The proposed testing methodology effectively simulates multidimensional extreme conditions.
important_claims.evidence: Experimental results show the method can replicate low pressure, intense ultraviolet radiation, wide temperature fluctuations, and strong winds, while also reducing testing cycle duration.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The developed testing methodology and retrofitting approach can be used by researchers and engineers to evaluate and improve the reliability of power transmission equipment in ultra-high altitude regions, such as the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau.

keynotes.title: Consensus-based feature selection and Bayesian-optimized stacking ensembles for comprehensive fault diagnosis in series-compensated ultra-high-voltage power systems

keynote.title: Consensus-based feature selection and Bayesian-optimized stacking ensembles for comprehensive fault diagnosis in series-compensated ultra-high-voltage power systems
keynote.core_problem: Accurate fault diagnosis in series-compensated ultra-high-voltage power systems, which is challenging due to complex data patterns, various potential faults, and data scarcity.
contributions: A simulation-based analysis for fault detection, classification, and localization as unified Multi-Classification tasks.
Improvement of time-frequency domain data through meta-feature engineering and consensus-based feature selection.
Model learning using Bayesian optimization within a Stacking Ensemble, integrating multiple machine learning models.
methods: Meta-feature engineering to improve time-frequency domain data.
Consensus-based feature selection combining gradient boosting, variance thresholding, and mutual information.

Bayesian optimization for model learning within a Stacking Ensemble that includes Extra Trees, Light Gradient Boosting Machine, and Extreme Gradient Boosting with an optimized Random Forest meta-classifier.
experiments_or_evidence: Evaluation of the accuracy, computational time, inference speed, and memory usage under challenging conditions.
Comparison of the performance of different models (Stacking Ensemble, Extra Trees, Light Gradient Boosting Machine, and Extreme Gradient Boosting) in terms of accuracy and computational demand.
limitations: The Stacking Ensemble has a higher computational demand compared to other models.
Extra Trees offers fast execution but shows decreased accuracy with increased classification complexity.

gap_signals.text: Extra Trees offers fast execution but shows decreased accuracy with increased classification complexity.
The Stacking Ensemble has a higher computational demand compared to other models.
Complex data patterns in electrical signals from ultra-high-voltage transmission lines with series compensation present challenges for accurate fault diagnosis.
Data scarcity owing to privacy and cybersecurity constraints presents a challenge for accurate fault diagnosis.

important_claims.claim: The Stacking Ensemble achieves the highest performance despite its greater computational demand.
important_claims.evidence: The results indicate that the Stacking Ensemble outperforms other models in terms of accuracy, even though it requires more computational resources.

important_claims.claim: Light Gradient Boosting Machine and Extreme Gradient Boosting demonstrate optimized performance across tasks owing to enhanced data processing.
important_claims.evidence: These models show balanced performance in terms of accuracy and computational efficiency, making them suitable for practical applications.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The proposed methodology, including meta-feature engineering, consensus-based feature selection, and Bayesian-optimized stacking ensembles, can be applied to other domains requiring robust and accurate multi-classification in complex and data-scarce environments.

keynotes.title: Research on Wireless Power Transmission Technology and Its Application in Electrical Engineering

keynote.title: Research on Wireless Power Transmission Technology and Its Application in Electrical Engineering
keynote.core_problem: Improving the flexibility and safety of power supply in electrical engineering systems, especially in high-voltage isolation, complex environments, and for mobile devices.
contributions: Comprehensive and systematic sorting out of three types of wireless power supply mechanisms: electromagnetic induction, magnetic resonance coupling, and microwave transmission.
Building a wireless power supply system framework with magnetic resonance coupling as the core.
Deployment of the system in 110 kV smart substation scenarios.
methods: Magnetic resonance coupling for wireless power transmission
experiments_or_evidence: Test results show an average transmission efficiency of 78.6% at a coupling distance of 2.8 meters.
Controlled the power fluctuation rate within ±2.6%.
Reduced the annual interruption frequency to 3 times.

gap_signals.text: Wireless power transmission shows good engineering practice feasibility and expansion potential, indicating areas for further exploration and improvement.

important_claims.claim: The comprehensive operation stability and safety far exceed the traditional wired method.
important_claims.evidence: Test results showing high transmission efficiency, controlled power fluctuation, and reduced interruption frequency.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The study provides a practical example of implementing wireless power transmission in smart substations, which can be used as a reference for future research on improving power supply flexibility and safety in similar or other complex environments.

keynotes.title: Study on the Safety of Power Frequency Electro-Magnetic Exposure from Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines

keynote.title: Study on the Safety of Power Frequency Electro-Magnetic Exposure from Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines
keynote.core_problem: Investigating the safety and potential hazards of power frequency electromagnetic exposure from ultra-high voltage (550 kV) transmission lines.
contributions: Provided a detailed analysis of the electromagnetic exposure levels under a 550 kV ultra-high voltage transmission line using a 1:1 human body model.
methods: Utilized a 1:1 human body model to measure electromagnetic exposure at different locations under a 550 kV transmission line.
experiments_or_evidence: Measured the induced electric field strength and current density in various parts of the human body model.
Observed that the maximum induced electric field strength was 62.665 mV/m and the current density was 487.943 μA/m², which are below significant hazard thresholds.
assumptions: The human body's weak conductivity provides a shielding effect against electromagnetic exposure.
limitations: The study is based on a 1:1 human body model, which may not fully represent the variability in human body types and conditions.

gap_signals.text: The study is based on a 1:1 human body model, which may not fully represent the variability in human body types and conditions.
More studies are needed to understand the long-term effects of low-level electromagnetic exposure on human health.

datasets_or_materials: Data from measurements of electromagnetic exposure using a 1:1 human body model under a 550 kV transmission line.

important_claims.claim: Despite some exposure levels approaching or exceeding the 10 kV/m limit, the actual harm to humans remains relatively low due to the shielding effect of the human body.
important_claims.evidence: The maximum induced electric field strength and current density were found to be significantly lower than the significant hazard thresholds established in biomedical standards.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: This study can be used as a reference for further research on the safety of electromagnetic exposure from high-voltage transmission lines and the development of more accurate models to simulate human exposure.

keynotes.title: System optimization of long-distance energy transportation in China using ultra-high-voltage power transmission

keynote.title: System optimization of long-distance energy transportation in China using ultra-high-voltage power transmission
keynote.core_problem: The unbalanced distribution of energy sources and electrical demands in China, and the challenges associated with traditional methods of energy transportation (e.g., coal transportation) over long distances.
contributions: Developed a system optimization model for exploring the optimal pathway for long-distance energy transportation in China during the 13th Five-Year Plan (FYP) period from the perspective of minimizing the cost of an energy transportation system.
methods: Development of a system optimization model
experiments_or_evidence: Results show that UHV transmission will be increasingly adopted but coal transportation will still play an important role.
Eight UHV lines are suggested to be prioritized for construction and use during the 13th FYP period.
High technology learning effect, electricity demand, and investment costs of power plants would promote the adoption of UHV.

gap_signals.text: Further exploration into the factors promoting the adoption of UHV, such as high technology learning effect, electricity demand, and investment costs of power plants.

important_claims.claim: UHV transmission will be increasingly adopted in the 13th FYP period, but coal transportation will still play an important role.
important_claims.evidence: Results from the system optimization model.

important_claims.claim: Eight UHV lines are suggested to be built and put into use as a priority in the 13th FYP period.
important_claims.evidence: Recommendations based on the system optimization model's results.

important_claims.claim: High technology learning effect, electricity demand, and investment costs of power plants would promote the adoption of UHV.
important_claims.evidence: Analysis of the factors influencing the adoption of UHV.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The system optimization model and the insights gained from the study can be used by policymakers and researchers to inform decisions on the development and implementation of UHV projects, as well as to further explore the economic and technical feasibility of different energy transportation methods in China or similar contexts.

keynotes.title: Development of High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) Transmission Systems for Efficient Power Transfer

keynote.title: Development of High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC) Transmission Systems for Efficient Power Transfer
keynote.core_problem: Efficient and safe power transfer over long distances, incorporating renewable energy sources and enhancing grid stability.
contributions: Highlighting the importance of HVDC systems in the history of electricity engineering and their role in efficient power transmission.
Discussing the benefits of HVDC over AC systems, including lower power loss, higher stability, and better power density.
methods: Use of voltage source converters (VSCs) and line-commutated converters (LCCs) for power conversion between AC and DC.
Enhanced control algorithms and real-time monitoring software to ensure dynamic stability and easy integration with existing grid infrastructures.
assumptions: HVDC technology will continue to be developed towards higher efficiency, reduced cost, and improved fault tolerance.

gap_signals.text: Further progress in HVDC systems is expected to support the transition to more sustainable and resilient electricity power systems.

important_claims.claim: HVDC has a lower power loss compared with standard alternating current (AC) systems, is more stable on the grid, and has better power density.
important_claims.evidence: The text highlights these as key strengths of HVDC systems over AC systems.

important_claims.claim: HVDC can adopt renewable energy sources like wind power and solar energy to ease the transfer of power through modular and long-distance power transmission from remote generation sites to the center of consumption.
important_claims.evidence: The paper mentions the potential of HVDC in integrating renewable energy sources for efficient power transmission.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: This paper provides a comprehensive overview of the development and advantages of HVDC systems, which can serve as a foundational reference for researchers and engineers working on improving power transmission technologies, especially in the context of renewable energy integration and grid stability.

keynotes.title: Application of Integrated Optical Electric-Field Sensor on the Measurements of Transient Voltages in AC High-Voltage Power Grids

keynote.title: Application of Integrated Optical Electric-Field Sensor on the Measurements of Transient Voltages in AC High-Voltage Power Grids
keynote.core_problem: Traditional measuring equipment for transient voltages in power grids has limited bandwidth and response speed, and is also large and heavy.
contributions: Development of an integrated optical electric-field sensor based on the Pockels effect for non-contact measurement of transient voltages.
Demonstration of the sensor's fast response speed (faster than 6 ns) and wide bandwidth (5 Hz to 100 MHz).
Measurement of lightning and switching transient voltages in high-voltage power grids.
methods: Use of the Pockels effect in an optical sensor for electric field sensing.
Deployment of the sensor in a 220 kV overhead transmission line and during the commissioning of a 500 kV power grid.
experiments_or_evidence: Monitoring of 307 lightning transient voltages caused by induction stroke in a 220 kV overhead transmission line.
Measurement of three types of typical switching transient voltages in a 500 kV AC power grid with peak values reaching 1.73 times the rated voltage.

gap_signals.text: The characteristics of the measured voltage waveforms differ from the standard lightning impulse voltage proposed by IEC standards, indicating a need for updated or more flexible standards.

datasets_or_materials: Data from 307 recorded lightning transient voltages in a 220 kV overhead transmission line.
Data from switching transient voltages in a 500 kV AC power grid.

important_claims.claim: The new sensor can measure transient voltages with a response speed faster than 6 ns and a bandwidth from 5 Hz to 100 MHz.
important_claims.evidence: The sensor was used to monitor 307 lightning transient voltages and switching transients in a 500 kV AC power grid.

important_claims.claim: The sensor is significantly smaller and lighter compared to traditional equipment.
important_claims.evidence: The dimensions of the sensors are 18 mm by 18 mm by 48 mm, and they weigh only dozens of grams.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The sensor design and its application in measuring transient voltages can be valuable for researchers working on improving the accuracy and efficiency of high-voltage power grid monitoring and fault analysis.

keynotes.title: Comparing a Multi-Terminal High Voltage Direct Current and Unified Power Flow Control for Short Circuit Level Reduction in Iraqi 400 Kv Grid

keynote.core_problem: The transmission and distribution networks are unable to meet the increasing demand for electricity due to resource constraints, leading to significant power losses and challenges in maximum power transfer.

methods: Modeling and simulation using the Power System Simulator for Engineering (PSS/E) version 32 Package Program.
experiments_or_evidence: Integration of multi-terminal HVDC transmission resulted in a considerable decrease in short circuit levels and network power losses for most buses in the system, as well as a reduction in the loading on the transmission lines in the southern region of the ISHV grid.

gap_signals.text: More detailed analysis or comparison with other technologies could be beneficial.

important_claims.claim: MT-HVDC reduces short circuit levels more efficiently than UPFC.
important_claims.evidence: Simulation results showed that integrating multi-terminal HVDC transmission led to a greater reduction in short circuit levels and network power losses compared to UPFC.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The study provides valuable insights into the comparative benefits of MT-HVDC and UPFC for improving network performance, particularly in terms of reducing short circuit levels and network power losses. This can guide future research and practical implementations in similar grid systems.

keynotes.title: Research on a Four-Layer Intelligent Monitoring and Coordination Model for Ultra-High Voltage Transmission and Substation Stations in Xinjiang

keynote.title: Research on a Four-Layer Intelligent Monitoring and Coordination Model for Ultra-High Voltage Transmission and Substation Stations in Xinjiang
keynote.core_problem: The rapid expansion of Xinjiang's ultra-high voltage (UHV) power transmission and transformation network, including vast geographical areas, harsh environmental conditions, and a high proportion of renewable energy, poses unique challenges that require an advanced monitoring and coordination model.
contributions: Proposes a four-layer intelligent monitoring and collaborative framework for UHV digital substations.
methods: Integrates the perception layer, edge computing layer, intelligent analysis layer, and collaborative decision-making layer.

gap_signals.text: This framework provides a comprehensive technical and functional reference, but further studies are needed to validate its effectiveness in real-world scenarios.

important_claims.claim: The proposed framework aims to achieve multi-source data collection, real-time energy flow visualization, predictive maintenance, resilience assessment, and multi-station collaborative control.
important_claims.evidence: The framework is designed with specific layers: the perception layer for continuous monitoring, the edge computing layer for low-latency local processing and anomaly detection, the intelligent analysis layer for machine learning-based health indices, load forecasting, and operation optimization, and the collaborative decision-making layer for cross-station coordination, scheduling, and emergency management.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The proposed four-layer framework can serve as a foundational model for developing similar systems in other regions facing similar challenges, providing a structured approach to integrating advanced technologies in UHV power transmission and substation management.

keynotes.title: Research on Reactive Power and Voltage Layered and Partitioned Collaborative Control Strategy for Suppressing the Chain Evolution of Large Scale New Energy DC Transmission

keynote.title: Reactive Power and Voltage Layered and Partitioned Collaborative Control Strategy for Suppressing the Chain Evolution of Large Scale New Energy DC Transmission
keynote.core_problem: Transient voltage fluctuations in large scale new energy ultra-high voltage direct current (DC) transmission systems, leading to a chain evolution of high and low voltage crossing protection actions that disconnect from the grid, thereby impacting power grid safety.
contributions: Proposed a reactive power and voltage layered and partitioned collaborative control strategy for multiple reactive power sources.
Introduced an SVG high and low voltage open-loop control strategy to enhance the timeliness of control and suppress rapid transient voltage fluctuations.
methods: Layered and partitioned collaborative control strategy for reactive power sources in DC rectifier station, new energy cluster stations, and new energy collection stations.
SVG high and low voltage open-loop control strategy to address time delay issues in power electronic response control.
experiments_or_evidence: Simulation analysis of the dynamic support and rapid regulation effect provided by the rectifier station synchronous condenser and thermal power units in the near area of new energy cluster stations.
Verification of the proposed control strategy's effectiveness through simulation examples of actual power grids.

gap_signals.text: Further research could explore the integration of additional renewable energy sources and the impact on the overall system stability.

important_claims.claim: The proposed multi-reactive power source layered and partitioned collaborative control strategy effectively suppresses the cascading evolution problem of the transmission end power grid.
important_claims.evidence: Based on simulation examples of actual power grids, the effectiveness of the proposed strategy has been verified, improving the DC transmission capacity and promoting the consumption of new energy.

important_claims.claim: The SVG high and low voltage open-loop control strategy enhances the timeliness of control and effectively suppresses rapid transient voltage fluctuations caused by commutation failure.
important_claims.evidence: Compared with closed-loop PID control, the open-loop strategy addresses the time delay of SVG power electronic response control in new energy collection stations.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: This paper provides a novel approach to managing transient voltage fluctuations in large-scale new energy DC transmission systems, which can be adapted or extended to other similar systems. The methods and strategies presented can serve as a foundation for further research in enhancing the stability and reliability of power grids with significant renewable energy integration.

keynotes.title: Investigation into In Situ Computed Tomography Inspection Technology for Conductor Connection Structure of Ultra-High Voltage Gas-Insulated Transmission Lines

Ring-clamping mechanism

Specialized algorithm for data and imaging accuracy
