keynote.reuse_value_for_research: This work provides a foundation for further research into non-destructive testing methods and real-time monitoring systems for critical infrastructure, specifically in the context of ultra-high voltage gas-insulated transmission lines. The developed technology and algorithms can be adapted and improved for other similar applications.

keynotes.title: Study on Power Supply Reliability of Sending End Matching Power Access to Ultra-high Voltage AC and DC Hybrid Power Grid

keynote.title: Study on Power Supply Reliability of Sending End Matching Power Access to Ultra-high Voltage AC and DC Hybrid Power Grid
keynote.core_problem: The imbalance in the development of AC and DC projects, characterized by a 'strong DC and weak AC' system, affects the safe and stable operation of UHV power grids, particularly when integrating sending end matching thermal power units.
contributions: Analysis of the impact of high-power matching power supply on the stability of UHV AC and DC hybrid power grids.
Proposal of prevention and control measures to enhance the reliability of power supply.
methods: Simulation using Power System Analysis Software Package
experiments_or_evidence: Simulation analysis of the Xilinhot to Taizhou (Jiangsu Province) UHV DC transmission project

gap_signals.text: Further research may be needed to validate the proposed prevention and control measures in different scenarios or with additional real-world data.

datasets_or_materials: Xilinhot to Taizhou (Jiangsu Province) UHV DC transmission project data
code_or_implementation: Use of Power System Analysis Software Package for simulations

important_claims.claim: The integration of high-power matching power supplies into a UHV AC and DC hybrid grid can significantly affect the grid's stability.
important_claims.evidence: Simulation results from the Xilinhot to Taizhou UHV DC transmission project.

important_claims.claim: Specific prevention and control measures can improve the reliability of power supply in such systems.
important_claims.evidence: Proposed measures based on the simulation analysis.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: This study provides a framework for analyzing the impact of power supply integration on UHV AC and DC hybrid grids, and the proposed measures can serve as a reference for enhancing grid reliability in similar contexts.

keynotes.title: A Simulation Study on Grounding Faults in the Metal Shield Layer of High-Voltage Power Cables

keynote.title: A Simulation Study on Grounding Faults in the Metal Shield Layer of High-Voltage Power Cables
keynote.core_problem: Understanding and diagnosing short-circuit faults in the metallic shielding layer of high-voltage power cables, which are critical for grid security and power supply quality.
contributions: Investigation of the mechanisms and characteristics of short-circuit faults in cable shields.
Development of a refined cable model incorporating frequency-dependent parameters using PSCAD software.
Analysis of the variation patterns of shield currents and zero-sequence currents under different operating conditions.
methods: Electromagnetic transient simulation using PSCAD software.
Construction of a refined cable model with frequency-dependent parameters.
experiments_or_evidence: Simulation results showing the electrical characteristics and influencing factors of grounding faults in the metal shield layer.

gap_signals.text: The study uses a refined cable model with frequency-dependent parameters, suggesting that previous models may not have been as accurate or detailed.

code_or_implementation: Use of PSCAD software for electromagnetic transient simulation.

important_claims.claim: The study provides both theoretical and empirical support for the rapid diagnosis and condition assessment in engineering applications.
important_claims.evidence: Analysis of the variation patterns of shield currents and zero-sequence currents under different operating conditions.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The refined cable model and simulation methodology can be reused for further studies on high-voltage power cable faults, enhancing the understanding and reliability of power transmission systems.

keynotes.title: Synoptic Characteristics of a Continuous Ice Covering Event on Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines in 2023

keynote.title: Synoptic Characteristics of a Continuous Ice Covering Event on Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines in 2023
keynote.core_problem: Understanding the synoptic causes of a large-scale continuous ice covering event on ultra-high voltage transmission lines in Yunnan, China, to prevent future disruptions.
contributions: Comprehensive analysis of the synoptic causes of ice covering on power lines using reanalysis data, meteorological observation data, and ice-covering records.
methods: Use of reanalysis data from the European Center for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
Observation data from a network of meteorological observation stations
Analysis of ice-covering records from ultra-high voltage transmission lines
experiments_or_evidence: The maximum ice covering thickness was 39.71mm during the event.
The Okhotsk Sea cold vortex and transverse trough were key factors in the glaciating process.
The blocking high and northerly dry and cold air movement southward influenced the icing process.

The interaction between warm and wet air from the western Pacific tropical high and the southern branch trough, and the cold air, led to the formation of overcooled raindrops or fog drops that froze on the wires.
assumptions: The reanalysis and observational data accurately represent the weather conditions during the event.
The ice-covering records from the power grid are reliable and comprehensive.
limitations: The study is focused on a specific event and may not fully generalize to all ice covering events.
The analysis is based on a combination of reanalysis and observational data, which may have inherent uncertainties.

gap_signals.text: The analysis is based on a combination of reanalysis and observational data, which may have inherent uncertainties.
The study is focused on a specific event and may not fully generalize to all ice covering events.
The study relies on a combination of reanalysis and observational data, which may have inherent uncertainties.
The findings are specific to the event in Qujing, Yunnan, and may not be directly applicable to other regions or different climatic conditions.

datasets_or_materials: Ice-covering records from ultra-high voltage transmission lines

important_claims.claim: The Okhotsk Sea cold vortex and the blocking high played significant roles in the ice covering process.
important_claims.evidence: The Okhotsk Sea cold vortex was maintained, and the blocking high led to the southward movement of northerly dry and cold air, contributing to the icing conditions.

important_claims.claim: The advance and retreat of the quasi-stationary front determined the icing process of the wire.
important_claims.evidence: When the cold air was strong and full of water vapor, the overcooled raindrops or fog drops condensed and froze on the wires, leading to the ice covering.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The methods and findings can be used to improve understanding and prediction of ice covering events on power lines, potentially aiding in the development of better mitigation strategies.

keynotes.title: Analysis of Lightning Strike Faults in Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines and Research on Treatment Measures

keynote.title: Analysis of Lightning Strike Faults in Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Lines and Research on Treatment Measures
keynote.core_problem: Understanding and mitigating the causes of lightning faults in ultra-high voltage transmission lines to reduce their frequency.
contributions: Identification of the primary causes of lightning faults in ultra-high voltage transmission lines
Proposal of six governance measures to avoid lightning faults
methods: Discharge traces and channels analysis
Grounding resistance detection
Lightning resistance level simulation
Lightning resistance level calculation

gap_signals.text: The paper does not detail the specific findings or the effectiveness of the proposed governance measures, suggesting a need for more comprehensive studies.

important_claims.claim: The paper identifies key factors contributing to lightning faults in ultra-high voltage transmission lines.
important_claims.evidence: The methods used include discharge traces and channels, grounding resistance detection, and simulations and calculations of lightning resistance levels.

important_claims.claim: Six governance measures are proposed to mitigate lightning faults.
important_claims.evidence: These measures are intended to provide a reference for reducing the frequency of such faults, though their specifics and effectiveness are not detailed.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The analytical framework and the proposed governance measures can serve as a foundation for further research into lightning fault mitigation in ultra-high voltage transmission lines, potentially leading to more robust and practical solutions.

keynotes.title: Research on the Protection Scheme of a High-Speed Railway Crossing 1000 KV Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Line

keynote.title: Research on the Protection Scheme of a High-Speed Railway Crossing 1000 KV Ultra-High Voltage Transmission Line
keynote.core_problem: Ensuring safety and operational integrity when a high-speed railway intersects with a 1000 kV ultra-high voltage transmission line, particularly under extreme conditions that may cause damage to the transmission cables.
contributions: Proposed a shed structure form and construction method for electric power protection.
Ensured safe operation of the high-speed railway and rapid construction in the safety zone near the transmission line.
methods: Utilized engineering examples to examine the protection structure scheme, protection distance, protection load, and construction procedures.
experiments_or_evidence: The results indicated that the concrete shed structure with a straight wall and a flat roof meets the requirements for high-speed railway crossings.
The line-break protection method enables the construction of an automatic warning protection corridor and a complete movable trolley quickly and safely.

gap_signals.text: It can be used as a reference point for other projects of a similar nature.

important_claims.claim: The proposed shed structure and construction method ensure the safe operation of the high-speed railway and rapid construction in the safety zone near the transmission line.
important_claims.evidence: Engineering examples and the implementation effect are positive, enabling the construction of an automatic warning protection corridor and a complete movable trolley quickly and safely.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The proposed shed structure and construction method can serve as a reference for future projects involving the intersection of high-speed railways and ultra-high voltage transmission lines, providing a practical solution for ensuring safety and operational integrity.

keynotes.title: A Scheme of Controlled Switching System in Converter Station

keynote.title: A Scheme of Controlled Switching System in Converter Station
keynote.core_problem: Circuit breaker closing resistor failures in HVDC and UHVDC projects, affecting the safety and stability of operation.
contributions: Introduction of a controlled switching system to improve the on/off transient process of circuit breakers and mitigate the impact on power equipment and grid when the closing resistor is removed.
Presentation of a special transformation scheme that eliminates the closing resistor and adds controlled switching, which has been implemented and proven effective in several projects.
methods: Implementation and testing of the controlled switching system in converter stations
Summarization of control strategies, commissioning requirements, and key engineering and operational issues

gap_signals.text: The need for a more reliable method to handle circuit breaker operations without the use of closing resistors, indicating a gap in current technology.

important_claims.claim: The controlled switching system can improve the on/off transient process of circuit breakers and avoid impacts on power equipment and the grid.
important_claims.evidence: Based on implementation and testing experience, the system has shown effectiveness in several projects.

important_claims.claim: The special transformation scheme of eliminating the closing resistor and adding controlled switching is effective.
important_claims.evidence: This scheme has been implemented in several projects and proven to be effective.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: Provides a practical approach and reference for implementing controlled switching systems in HVDC and UHVDC projects, enhancing the reliability and performance of circuit breaker operations.

keynotes.title: Review of Power Electronic Solutions for Dielectric Barrier Discharge Applications

keynote.title: Review of Power Electronic Solutions for Dielectric Barrier Discharge Applications
keynote.core_problem: Bridging the gap between DBD applications and power electronics design, specifically addressing the need for optimized power supply topologies that meet application-specific requirements.
contributions: Comprehensive review of DBD power supply topologies
Examination of the dependence of the DBD electrical model on reactor geometry
Analysis of application-driven requirements for waveform characteristics
Systematic review of sinusoidal and pulsed power supplies, including performance trade-offs
methods: Literature review
Categorization and comparison of power supply topologies
assumptions: The performance and applicability of DBD systems are significantly influenced by the power supply topology.

gap_signals.text: Untested topologies and emerging applications in DBD power supply design

important_claims.claim: The choice of power supply topology is crucial for optimizing DBD system performance.
important_claims.evidence: The paper reviews various topologies and their suitability based on specific application requirements, such as waveform characteristics and reactor geometry.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: Provides a structured overview and guidance for researchers and engineers developing high-performance DBD power supplies, highlighting key considerations and future research directions.

keynotes.title: Multi‐Layer Charge Regulation Engineering for Efficient Enhancement of Aramid Epoxy‐Based Composite at Withstand Voltage Performance

keynote.title: Multi‐Layer Charge Regulation Engineering for Efficient Enhancement of Aramid Epoxy‐Based Composite at Withstand Voltage Performance
keynote.core_problem: Improving the withstand voltage performance of aramid epoxy-based composites to ensure the safe and stable operation of high-voltage transmission and transformation equipment.
contributions: Designed a multi-layer charge regulation engineering on the aramid fiber's surface to enhance the withstand voltage of the composites.
methods: Utilized the charge trapping characteristics of insulating materials and the charge transport properties of semiconductor materials.
Combined charge dissipation and trap distribution, interface bond strength, molecular dynamics simulations, and phase-field modeling to understand the mechanism.
experiments_or_evidence: Tests of flashover voltage and breakdown strength were conducted to validate the effectiveness of the multi-layer charge regulation engineering.
After constructing the optimal multi-layer charge regulation structure, the flashover voltage increased by 62.03%, and the breakdown strength improved by 65.22%.

gap_signals.text: The paper does not discuss any limitations or potential areas for further research, suggesting that there may be gaps in understanding the long-term stability and practical implementation of the multi-layer charge regulation engineering.

important_claims.claim: The multi-layer charge regulation engineering significantly enhances the withstand voltage of aramid epoxy-based composites.
important_claims.evidence: Flashover voltage increased by 62.03%, and breakdown strength improved by 65.22% after applying the multi-layer charge regulation structure.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: This work provides a novel approach to enhancing the electrical properties of composite materials, which can be applied to other types of insulating materials and potentially improve the design of high-voltage equipment. The combination of experimental validation and simulation results offers a comprehensive method for future research in this area.

keynotes.title: Testing Methods for Ultra-High Altitude Environmental Adaptability of Power Transmission and Transformation Sensing Terminals

keynote.title: Testing Methods for Ultra-High Altitude Environmental Adaptability of Power Transmission and Transformation Sensing Terminals
keynote.core_problem: Power transmission and transformation sensing terminals face critical challenges in ultra-high altitude extreme environments, including material performance degradation, abnormal electrical characteristics, and inadequate operational adaptability. Existing environmental testing standards are limited in simulating multi-stress coupled scenarios.
contributions: Proposed an ultra-high altitude adaptability testing methodology for power transmission sensing terminals based on multidimensional environmental coupling loading and dynamic gradient control.
methods: Retrofitting a comprehensive test chamber with integrated ultraviolet irradiation, air circulation, and low-pressure control modules to establish a synchronized multi-environmental factor loading system.
Implementation of a pressure-temperature-ultraviolet linkage algorithm coupled with a dynamic gradient control model to simulate altitude gradient variations.
experiments_or_evidence: Experimental results demonstrate the method's effectiveness in replicating multidimensional extreme conditions: low pressure (≤70 kPa), intense ultraviolet radiation (≥1000 W/m2), wide temperature fluctuations (-40°C to 70°C), and strong winds (≥15 m/s).
The proposed method significantly reduces testing cycle duration.

gap_signals.text: Existing environmental testing standards demonstrate limitations in simulating multi-stress coupled scenarios due to insufficient simulation capabilities and outdated control strategies.

important_claims.claim: The proposed method can effectively replicate multidimensional extreme conditions.
important_claims.evidence: Experimental results show that the method can replicate conditions such as low pressure (≤70 kPa), intense ultraviolet radiation (≥1000 W/m2), wide temperature fluctuations (-40°C to 70°C), and strong winds (≥15 m/s).

important_claims.claim: The new testing methodology significantly reduces testing cycle duration.
important_claims.evidence: Experimental results indicate a reduction in the time required to complete the testing cycles.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: This research provides technical support for reliability evaluation of power transmission equipment in ultra-high altitude regions, such as the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau, and demonstrates substantial engineering application value.

keynotes.title: Research on Wireless Power Transmission Technology and Its Application in Electrical Engineering

keynote.title: Research on Wireless Power Transmission Technology and Its Application in Electrical Engineering
keynote.core_problem: Improving the flexibility, safety, and efficiency of power supply in electrical engineering systems, especially in challenging environments such as high-voltage isolation, complex terrains, and for mobile devices.
contributions: Comprehensive and systematic review of three types of wireless power supply mechanisms: electromagnetic induction, magnetic resonance coupling, and microwave transmission.
Development of a wireless power supply system framework with magnetic resonance coupling at its core.
Deployment and testing of the system in a 110 kV smart substation environment.
methods: Magnetic resonance coupling as the primary method for the wireless power supply system.
experiments_or_evidence: Achieved an average transmission efficiency of 78.6% at a coupling distance of 2.8 meters.
Controlled power fluctuation rate within ±2.6%.
Reduced the annual interruption frequency to 3 times.

gap_signals.text: No explicit limitations or gaps mentioned, but the potential for expansion and further application is highlighted, suggesting areas for future research.

important_claims.claim: The wireless power supply system achieves high efficiency and stability.
important_claims.evidence: Test results show an average transmission efficiency of 78.6% and a power fluctuation rate controlled within ±2.6%.

important_claims.claim: The system significantly reduces the annual interruption frequency compared to traditional wired methods.
important_claims.evidence: Annual interruption frequency reduced to 3 times, indicating better operation stability and safety.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The study provides a practical example of deploying wireless power transmission technology in a real-world setting, which can be used as a reference for similar applications in other electrical engineering contexts. The focus on magnetic resonance coupling also highlights its effectiveness, offering a valuable case study for researchers and engineers working on wireless power solutions.

keynotes.title: System optimization of long-distance energy transportation in China using ultra-high-voltage power transmission

keynote.title: System optimization of long-distance energy transportation in China using ultra-high-voltage power transmission
keynote.core_problem: Unbalanced distribution of energy sources and electrical demands in China, leading to challenges in traditional methods of energy transportation such as coal.
contributions: Development of a system optimization model for exploring the optimal pathway for long-distance energy transportation in China during the 13th Five-Year Plan (FYP) period.
methods: Use of an optimization model to evaluate different energy transportation pathways with a focus on cost minimization.
experiments_or_evidence: Analysis results showing that UHV transmission will be increasingly adopted but coal transportation remains important, and eight specific UHV lines are recommended for priority construction.

gap_signals.text: The high technology learning effect, electricity demand, and investment costs of power plants would promote the adoption of UHV, indicating areas for further investigation.

important_claims.claim: UHV transmission will be increasingly adopted in the 13th FYP period, but coal transportation will still play an important role.
important_claims.evidence: Results from the system optimization model.

important_claims.claim: Eight UHV lines are suggested to be built and put into use as a priority in the 13th FYP period.
important_claims.evidence: Results from the system optimization model.

keynote.reuse_value_for_research: The developed system optimization model can be adapted or extended for analyzing other regions or time periods, providing a framework for similar studies on energy transportation systems.

Abstract: As core equipment for grid condition monitoring, power transmission and transformation sensing terminals face critical challenges in ultra-high altitude extreme environments, including material performance degradation, abnormal electrical characteristics, and inadequate operational adaptab...[truncated]
Full text excerpt:
Existing environmental testing standards demonstrate limitations in simulating multi-stress coupled scenarios due to insufficient simulation capabilities and outdated control strategies.

This paper proposes an ultra-high altitude adaptability testing methodology for power transmission sensing terminals based on multidimensional environmental coupling loading and dynamic gradient control.
The implementation of a pressure-temperature-ultraviolet linkage algorithm coupled with a dynamic gradient control model enables precise simulation of altitude gradient variations.

Experimental results demonstrate the method's effectiveness in replicating multidimensional extreme conditions: low pressure (≤70 kPa), intense ultraviolet radiation (≥1000 W/m2), wide temperature fluctuations (-40°C to 70°C), and strong winds (≥15 m/s), while significantly reducing testing cycle du...[truncated]

evidence_type_annotations.claim: consensus-based feature selection and Bayesian-optimized stacking ensembles
a fault diagnosis framework using consensus-based feature selection and Bayesian-optimized stacking ensembles, which demonstrates high performance in fault detection, classification, and localization

Abstract: Modern power systems incorporate ultra-high-voltage transmission lines with series compensation to enhance their reliability and stability. These configurations generate complex data patterns in electrical signals, which present challenges for accurate fault diagnosis. This complexity aris...[truncated]
Full text excerpt:

The methodology improves the time-frequency domain data through meta-feature engineering and utilizes consensus-based feature selection by combining gradient boosting, variance thresholding, and mutual information.
The evaluation considers the accuracy, computational time, inference speed, and memory usage under challenging conditions to address practical limitations.
The results indicate that the Stacking Ensemble achieves the highest performance despite its greater computational demand.
Extra Trees offers the fastest execution but shows decreased accuracy with increased classification complexity.
These findings confirm the practical applicability of the proposed fault diagnosis framework to modern ultra-high-voltage power systems.

standardization work for SSCS is an ongoing process and cannot be achieved in one effort
Full text excerpt:

Aligned with China’s "dual carbon" strategy and the technical demands of New Power Systems, this revision introduces expanded testing scopes, enhanced network security protection requirements, and refined dynamic simulation methodologies.
The revised standard establishes a robust framework for standardized testing, ensuring reliability and adaptability of SSCS in high-renewable, power electronics-dominated grids.

From the main aspects of this revision and the results of engineering empirical tests, the testing methods and technical requirements for SSCS should evolve iteratively in sync with the development process and technological advancements of power systems.

Experimental results demonstrate the method's effectiveness in replicating multidimensional extreme conditions: low pressure (≤70 kPa), intense ultraviolet radiation (≥1000 W/m2), wide temperature fluctuations (-40°C to 70°C), and strong winds (≥15 m/s), while significantly reducing testing cycle du...[truncated]

a fault diagnosis framework using consensus-based feature selection and Bayesian-optimized stacking ensembles, which demonstrates high performance in fault detection, classification, and localization

Abstract: Modern power systems incorporate ultra-high-voltage transmission lines with series compensation to enhance their reliability and stability. These configurations generate complex data patterns in electrical signals, which present challenges for accurate fault diagnosis. This complexity aris...[truncated]
Full text excerpt:

The methodology improves the time-frequency domain data through meta-feature engineering and utilizes consensus-based feature selection by combining gradient boosting, variance thresholding, and mutual information.
The evaluation considers the accuracy, computational time, inference speed, and memory usage under challenging conditions to address practical limitations.
The results indicate that the Stacking Ensemble achieves the highest performance despite its greater computational demand.
Extra Trees offers the fastest execution but shows decreased accuracy with increased classification complexity.
These findings confirm the practical applicability of the proposed fault diagnosis framework to modern ultra-high-voltage power systems.

socratic_question_reports.thought: DuZhi generated structured Socratic questions targeting definitions, constraints, causal links, evidence gaps, and counterexamples.

questions.question: Can you rewrite the hypothesis as an explicit input -> mechanism -> output chain with evidence for each arrow?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: A single broad sentence hides missing causal links and prevents targeted revision.
questions.required_revision: Provide a three-to-five-step causal chain and cite or label the evidence for each link.

questions.question: For the evidence-derived terms multi-layer charge regulation engineering, aramid epoxy-based composite, flashover voltage, breakdown strength, simulating multi-stress coupled scenarios due to insufficient simulation, which exact term sits at the intervention, mediator, and output positions of the causal chain?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: Domain-targeted causal probing forces MingLi to connect the hypothesis to field-specific entities without hardcoding the field.
questions.required_revision: Rewrite the causal chain using at least two evidence-derived terms and mark unsupported links as assumptions.

questions.question: What is the nearest domain-standard baseline or negative control that would make the claim nontrivial?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: Without a baseline, the hypothesis cannot distinguish genuine mechanism from general performance drift.
questions.required_revision: Name at least one domain-standard baseline and one failure-mode or negative control.

questions.question: Which part of the hypothesis is directly measurable, and which part is inferred from those measurements?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: AHOIS-style clarification requires separating observables from inferred mechanisms before testing.
questions.required_revision: Add explicit observables, inferred constructs, and the mapping between them.

questions.question: Under what validity regime is the mechanism expected to hold, and where should it fail?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: Unstated boundary conditions are a common CAWM risk under regime shift.
questions.required_revision: Add explicit assumptions, validity range, and at least one expected failure condition.

questions.question: Which sentence or result in the imported PaperGraph evidence grounds the strongest mechanistic premise?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: ARIS-style evidence gates require claim-to-source traceability, not just thematic similarity.
questions.required_revision: Map each central premise to a PaperGraph citation or mark it as speculative.

questions.question: The evidence repeatedly mentions multi-layer charge regulation engineering, aramid epoxy-based composite, flashover voltage, breakdown strength, simulating multi-stress coupled scenarios due to insufficient simulation, power transmission and transformation sensing terminals in ultra-high altitude extreme environments; which of these domain-specific constraints is actually required for the mechanism to hold?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: A strong critique should test the hypothesis against the concrete variables found in the retrieved literature, not only generic stress tests.
questions.required_revision: Name the required evidence-derived constraint, its allowed range or qualitative regime, and how it will be monitored.

questions.question: What data, instrument, simulation, or experimental platform can actually observe the claimed causal step?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: A hypothesis can be conceptually attractive but infeasible if the decisive mechanism is not observable.
questions.required_revision: Specify the observation platform and feasibility constraint for the decisive causal link.

questions.question: What outcome should occur if data_distribution: training/reference distribution -> out-of-distribution or low-data setting, and would that falsify the mechanism or only weaken it?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: Counterexamples reveal whether the mechanism has real explanatory content across regimes.
questions.required_revision: Add predicted behavior under this shifted condition and define pass/fail interpretation.

questions.question: What outcome should occur if noise_level: nominal signal quality -> high-noise or missing-data condition, and would that falsify the mechanism or only weaken it?
questions.target_claim: No finalized hypothesis was available; critique the failed MingLi finalization and required evidence gates.
questions.why_it_matters: Counterexamples reveal whether the mechanism has real explanatory content across regimes.
